(b), which denied the deduction for certain Federal income taxes, for Federal war profits and excess profits taxes, import duties, excise and stamp taxes, and estate, inheritance, legacy, succession and gift taxes, local assessments against benefits increasing property values, and certain taxes imposed by any foreign country or possession of the United States if the taxpayer chose to benefit by section 901 relating to foreign tax credit, and for taxes on real property to the extent that they are treated as imposed on another taxpayer, was struck out.Pub. L. 88–272, § 207(a)Subsec. (c). , substituted provisions denying the deduction for taxes assessed against local benefits which increase property value, except for so much as is properly allocable to maintenance or interest charges, and for real property taxes to the extent they are treated as imposed on another taxpayer, for provisions relating to certain retail sales taxes and gasoline taxes, the extent to which they were deductible, and to definition of “state or local sales tax”.Pub. L. 88–272, § 207(a)Subsec. (f). , inserted “State” before “real property taxes”.Pub. L. 88–272, § 207(b)(1)Subsec. (g). , designated existing provisions as par. (1), substituted “1451” for “1451(f)” and added par. (2).Pub. L. 88–272, § 207(b)(2)1958—Subsecs. (f), (g). , added subsec. (f) and redesignated former subsec. (f) as (g).Pub. L. 85–866, § 6(a)

children.text: State and local, and foreign, real property taxes.
State and local personal property taxes.
State and local, and foreign, income, war profits, and excess profits taxes.
The GST tax imposed on income distributions.

children.heading: Personal property taxes
children.label: 1 Personal property taxes
children.text: The term “personal property tax” means an ad valorem tax which is imposed on an annual basis in respect of personal property.

children.heading: State or local taxes
children.label: 2 State or local taxes
children.text: A State or local tax includes only a tax imposed by a State, a possession of the United States, or a political subdivision of any of the foregoing, or by the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Foreign taxes
children.label: 3 Foreign taxes
children.text: A foreign tax includes only a tax imposed by the authority of a foreign country.

children.label: i
children.text: the tax imposed by section 2601, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any State tax described in section 2604 (as in effect before its repeal),

children.heading: Special rule for tax paid before due date
children.label: B Special rule for tax paid before due date
children.text: Any tax referred to in subparagraph (A) imposed with respect to a transfer occurring during the taxable year of the distributee (or, in the case of a taxable termination, the trust) which is paid not later than the time prescribed by law (including extensions) for filing the return with respect to such transfer shall be treated as having been paid on the last day of the taxable year in which the transfer was made.

children.heading: Election to deduct State and local sales taxes in lieu of State and local income taxes
children.label: A Election to deduct State and local sales taxes in lieu of State and local income taxes
i
children.text: without regard to the reference to State and local income taxes, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as if State and local general sales taxes were referred to in a paragraph thereof.

children.heading: Definition of general sales tax
children.label: B Definition of general sales tax
children.text: The term “general sales tax” means a tax imposed at one rate with respect to the sale at retail of a broad range of classes of items.

children.heading: Special rules for food, etc.
children.label: C Special rules for food, etc.
i
children.text: the fact that the tax does not apply with respect to some or all of such items shall not be taken into account in determining whether the tax applies with respect to a broad range of classes of items, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the fact that the rate of tax applicable with respect to some or all of such items is lower than the general rate of tax shall not be taken into account in determining whether the tax is imposed at one rate.

children.heading: Items taxed at different rates
children.label: D Items taxed at different rates
children.text: Except in the case of a lower rate of tax applicable with respect to an item described in subparagraph (C), no deduction shall be allowed under this paragraph for any general sales tax imposed with respect to an item at a rate other than the general rate of tax.

children.label: i
children.text: is imposed on the use, storage, or consumption of such item, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is complementary to a general sales tax, but only if a deduction is allowable under this paragraph with respect to items sold at retail in the taxing jurisdiction which are similar to such item.

children.heading: Special rule for motor vehicles
children.label: F Special rule for motor vehicles
children.text: In the case of motor vehicles, if the rate of tax exceeds the general rate, such excess shall be disregarded and the general rate shall be treated as the rate of tax.

children.heading: Separately stated general sales taxes
children.label: G Separately stated general sales taxes
children.text: If the amount of any general sales tax is separately stated, then, to the extent that the amount so stated is paid by the consumer (other than in connection with the consumer’s trade or business) to the seller, such amount shall be treated as a tax imposed on, and paid by, such consumer.

children.heading: Amount of deduction may be determined under tables
children.label: H Amount of deduction may be determined under tables
I
children.text: the amount determined under this paragraph (without regard to this subparagraph) with respect to motor vehicles, boats, and other items specified by the Secretary, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount determined under tables prescribed by the Secretary with respect to items to which subclause (I) does not apply.

children.label: I
children.text: shall reflect the provisions of this paragraph,

children.label: II
children.text: shall be based on the average consumption by taxpayers on a State-by-State basis (as determined by the Secretary) of items to which clause (i)(I) does not apply, taking into account filing status, number of dependents, adjusted gross income, and rates of State and local general sales taxation, and

children.label: III
children.text: need only be determined with respect to adjusted gross incomes up to the applicable amount (as determined under section 68(b) ).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Limitation on individual deductions for taxable years 2018 through 2025
children.label: 6 Limitation on individual deductions for taxable years 2018 through 2025
A
children.text: foreign real property taxes shall not be taken into account under subsection (a)(1), and

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount of taxes taken into account under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a) and paragraph (5) of this subsection for any taxable year shall not exceed the applicable limitation amount (half the applicable limitation amount in the case of a married individual filing a separate return).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning in calendar year 2025, $40,000,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning in calendar year 2026, $40,400,

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning after calendar year 2026 and before 2030, 101 percent of the dollar amount in effect under this subparagraph for taxable years beginning in the preceding calendar year, and

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning after calendar year 2029, $10,000.

children.heading: Phasedown based on modified adjusted gross income
children.label: B Phasedown based on modified adjusted gross income

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (iii), in the case of any taxable year beginning before , the applicable limitation amount shall be reduced by 30 percent of the excess (if any) of the taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income over the threshold amount (half the threshold amount in the case of a married individual filing a separate return).January 1, 2030

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning in calendar year 2025, $500,000,

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning in calendar year 2026, $505,000, and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of any taxable year beginning after calendar year 2026, 101 percent of the dollar amount in effect under this subparagraph for taxable years beginning in the preceding calendar year.

children.heading: Limitation on reduction
children.label: iii Limitation on reduction
children.text: The reduction under clause (i) shall not result in the applicable limitation amount being less than $10,000.

children.heading: Modified adjusted gross income
children.label: iv Modified adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “modified adjusted gross income” means adjusted gross income increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.heading: Deduction denied in case of certain taxes
children.label: c Deduction denied in case of certain taxes

children.text: Taxes assessed against local benefits of a kind tending to increase the value of the property assessed; but this paragraph shall not prevent the deduction of so much of such taxes as is properly allocable to maintenance or interest charges.
Taxes on real property, to the extent that subsection (d) requires such taxes to be treated as imposed on another taxpayer.

children.heading: Apportionment of taxes on real property between seller and purchaser
children.label: d Apportionment of taxes on real property between seller and purchaser
A
children.text: so much of the real property tax as is properly allocable to that part of such year which ends on the day before the date of the sale shall be treated as a tax imposed on the seller, and

children.label: B
children.text: so much of such tax as is properly allocable to that part of such year which begins on the date of the sale shall be treated as a tax imposed on the purchaser.

children.label: i
children.text: a taxpayer may not, by reason of his method of accounting, deduct any amount for taxes unless paid, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the other party to the sale is (under the law imposing the real property tax) liable for the real property tax for the real property tax year,

children.label: i
children.text: is treated, under paragraph (1) of this subsection, as imposed on the taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not, by reason of the taxpayer’s method of accounting, be deducted by the taxpayer for any taxable year,

children.heading: Taxes of shareholder paid by corporation
children.label: e Taxes of shareholder paid by corporation

children.text: the deduction allowed by subsection (a) shall be allowed to the corporation; and
no deduction shall be allowed the shareholder for such tax.

children.heading: Deduction for one-half of self-employment taxes
children.label: f Deduction for one-half of self-employment taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of an individual, in addition to the taxes described in subsection (a), there shall be allowed as a deduction for the taxable year an amount equal to one-half of the taxes imposed by section 1401 (other than the taxes imposed by section 1401(b)(2)) for such taxable year.

children.heading: Deduction treated as attributable to trade or business
children.label: 2 Deduction treated as attributable to trade or business
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, the deduction allowed by paragraph (1) shall be treated as attributable to a trade or business carried on by the taxpayer which does not consist of the performance of services by the taxpayer as an employee.
For provisions disallowing any deduction for certain taxes, see section 275.
For treatment of taxes imposed by Indian tribal governments (or their subdivisions), see section 7871.
